title: Recent advances in Brill--Noether theory and the geometry of Brill--Noether curves
plain_text: af_smrc ] Let \(g, r, d, k\) be such that \(g-d+r 0\) and \(0 (g, r, d) < r-2\) and \(k 2\). Let \(C\) be a general curve of genus \(g\). Then the locus of line bundles \(L W^r_dC\) for which \(^k_L\) is not of maximal rank has dimension equation (g, r, d) - 1 - | r + k k - (dk+1 - g) |. equation In particular, \(^k_L\) is of maximal rank for every \(L W^r_dC\) when this quantity is negative.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A central, nontrivial generalization of the Maximal Rank Theorem in Brill–Noether theory; resolving it would yield broad structural consequences for BN-curves and moduli spaces (already used to detect general type).
viability_rationale: Active area with recent partial/tropical breakthroughs (specific cases) and Larson's Maximal Rank theorem, but the Strong Maximal Rank Conjecture remains very open in full generality with substantial technical gaps.
rationale: The Strong Maximal Rank Conjecture is stated as a conjecture and the text explicitly notes it "remains very much open in general," with only partial cases proved.
evidence_snippet: Lines 631–637: statement of the Strong Maximal Rank Conjecture. Line 641: "While Conjecture~\ref{conj:smrc} remains very much open in general..."

title: Symmetries and Higher-Form Connections in Derived Differential Geometry
plain_text: Let $H _ cl ^ n+1 (M)$ be the field strength of the $(n - 1)$-gerbe connection $$. There is a canonical equivalence equation Cour_ n-1 (H) ^ n-1 (g, ) equation of $L_$-algebras over $TM$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Identifies higher Courant L_infty-algebras with derived infinitesimal symmetries of gerbes; proven for n=2 and aligns with expectations—important structural bridge in higher/derived gauge theory but specialized.
viability_rationale: n=2 case proven and structural reduction theorems exist; community expectations and partial results suggest tractability, but higher-n technicalities and missing geometric interpretations limit near-term completion.
rationale: The authors formulate Conjecture~\ref{conj:Coursym} and provide evidence (true for n=2) but do not prove the general equivalence, so it remains an open conjecture in the paper.
evidence_snippet: Lines 5060–5066: statement of Conjecture~\ref{conj:Coursym}. Line 5069: "...the conjecture is true for n = 2."

title: Uniformity of extremal graph-codes
body_tex: For every pair $d,k$ of positive integers with $k\meg 2$ and every\, $0<\delta\mik 1$, there exists a positive integer $\mathrm{DPHJ}(d,k,\delta)$ such that if $n\meg \mathrm{DPHJ}(d,k,\delta)$, then any subset of $W(n,d,k)$ with cardinality at least $\delta\, k^{n^d}$ contains a polynomial combinatorial line of\, $W(n,d,k)$.
plain_text: For every pair $d,k$ of positive integers with $k 2$ and every\, $0< 1$, there exists a positive integer $DPHJ(d,k,)$ such that if $n DPHJ(d,k,)$, then any subset of $W(n,d,k)$ with cardinality at least $\, k^ n^d $ contains a polynomial combinatorial line of\, $W(n,d,k)$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: The density Polynomial Hales–Jewett conjecture (even the d=k=2 case) is a central, long-standing open problem in density Ramsey theory; resolving it would be a major breakthrough with wide consequences.
viability_rationale: Density Polynomial Hales--Jewett (even d=k=2) is a major open problem in density Ramsey theory; considered deep and unlikely to be resolved within five years.
rationale: This is the classical density Polynomial Hales–Jewett conjecture (Bergelson); the paper states the d=k=2 case is open and does not resolve it.
evidence_snippet: Lines 989-995: DPHJ conjecture: density polynomial Hales–Jewett statement; authors note even the first higher-dimensional case d=k=2 is open.

title: Phase transitions with bounded index: Parallels to De Giorgi's conjecture
body_tex:
\label{conj:indclasnogrowth}
    Let $4\leq n\leq 7$, and let $u:\R^n\to[-1,1]$ be a finite Morse index solution to the Allen--Cahn equation. Then, $u$ is one-dimensional.
plain_text: Let $4 n 7$, and let $u:^n$ be a finite Morse index solution to the Allen--Cahn equation. Then, $u$ is one-dimensional.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A finite‑index analogue of De Giorgi for 4≤n≤7: if true, it yields a strong rigidity classification across higher dimensions, impacting PDE, geometric measure theory, and phase transition theory.
viability_rationale: Significant recent progress (full result in R^4 with growth, conditional higher-dim results) yields a concrete path; removing growth/conditional hypotheses is plausible but nontrivial within five years.
rationale: The finite-index De Giorgi conjecture for 4≤n≤7 is stated, but the paper proves only the n=4 case with energy-growth assumption and a conditional result for 4≤n≤7 assuming a stable-classification hypothesis.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture stated (lines 478–481). Theorem: classification in R^4 requires E(u,B_R) ≤ C R^3; conditional classification for 4≤n≤7 assumes (★) stable-classification.

title: Equilibrium measures for higher dimensional rotationally symmetric Riesz gases
body_tex:
\label{Cj1}
The equilibrium measure is supported in $\R^d$ if and only if $a \ge a_{ \rm cri }$.
plain_text: The equilibrium measure is supported in $^d$ if and only if $a a_ cri $.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A threshold criterion for full support of the Coulomb half‑space equilibrium measure. It addresses a natural phase‑transition in Riesz/Coulomb gases with clear potential‑theoretic and probabilistic implications.
viability_rationale: Variational/analytic statement with proofs for low dimensions and a concrete reduction; accessible to current techniques and likely resolvable in the moderate term by experts.
rationale: Authors state the support-if-and-only-if claim for all d as a conjecture and explicitly note no rigorous proof in full generality, proving only the special case d=3 in the appendix.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture: equilibrium measure supported in R^d iff a ≥ a_cri. Authors: 'At present, we are not aware of a rigorous proof of this statement in full generality.' Appendix proves d=3.

title: Decidability of Interpretability
body_tex:
\label{conj:bp}
    Let $\bC$ be a first-order reduct of a finitely bounded homogeneous structure $\bA$. Then $\CSP(\bC)$ is either in P or NP-complete.
plain_text: Let $$ be a first-order reduct of a finitely bounded homogeneous structure $$. Then $()$ is either in P or NP-complete.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: The Bodirsky–Pinsker dichotomy is a central, field-level conjecture defining P vs NP-complete classification for a broad class of infinite-domain CSPs; resolving it would have major consequences across complexity and model theory.
viability_rationale: Major, well-known Bodirsky–Pinsker dichotomy: long-standing, widely studied with many partial results but still far from a general resolution; unlikely within five years.
rationale: The Bodirsky–Pinsker conjecture is stated and noted in the paper as still wide-open in general despite many special-case confirmations.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture stated (lines 152–154). Paper remarks it has been confirmed in many instances but remains "wide-open in general" (line 156).

title: Totally $Δ$-Modular Tree Decompositions of Graphic Matrices for Integer Programming
body_tex:
\label{conj:IPonaTree}
    Fix a positive integer $d$. Let $A$ be a matrix with exactly two nonzero entries per row such that $G(A)$ is a tree and $\|A\|_\infty \leq d$. Then the integer program
    \[\max\{w^Tx : Ax \leq b, x \in [\ell,u] \cap \mathbb{Z}^n\}\]
    can be solved in polynomial time for constant $d$.
plain_text: Fix a positive integer $d$. Let $A$ be a matrix with exactly two nonzero entries per row such that $G(A)$ is a tree and $\|A\|_ d$. Then the integer program \[\ w^Tx : Ax b, x [,u] Z^n\ \] can be solved in polynomial time for constant $d$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Polynomial-time solvability for two-nonzero-per-row matrices whose support graph is a tree (bounded entries) is a focused, technical open question; interesting for the subfield but narrowly scoped.
viability_rationale: Specialized, narrower case (two nonzeros per row, tree support, bounded entries). Unknown even for paths, but concentrated structure and active techniques give moderate chance of near-term resolution.
rationale: The paper explicitly labels this tree/path integer-program solvability statement as an open conjecture and remarks it is unknown even for a path.
evidence_snippet: Lines 896–903: conjecture stated and authors note it is not known even when G(A) is a path.

title: Acyclic sets and colorings in digraphs under restrictions on degrees and cycle lengths
body_tex:
For every tournament $H$, there is $\varepsilon_H > 0$ such that 
any $H$-free tournament $T$ on $n$ vertices satisfies $\vec{\alpha}(T) \geq n^{\varepsilon_H}$.
plain_text: For every tournament $H$, there is $_H > 0$ such that any $H$-free tournament $T$ on $n$ vertices satisfies $(T) n^ _H $.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Equivalent to the Erdős–Hajnal conjecture for tournaments; resolving it would settle a central, widely influential extremal-combinatorics problem with broad implications.
viability_rationale: Equivalent to the Erdős–Hajnal conjecture, a longstanding major open problem with no general approach; unlikely to be resolved in the next five years.
rationale: The tournament version is presented as a conjecture and is noted equivalent to the Erdős–Hajnal conjecture, which remains open in general; the paper does not prove it.
evidence_snippet: Lines 136–139: tournament conjecture stated. Lines 141–143: Alon, Pach and Solymosi proved equivalence to the Erdős–Hajnal conjecture (central open question).

title: Cohen-Macaulayness of squarefree powers of edge ideals of whisker graphs
body_tex:
Let $G = W(C_n)$ be the whisker graph of a cycle of length $n$. Then
\[
\depth\!\bigl(R/I(G)^{[q]}\bigr)
=
\begin{cases}
n+q-1, & \text{ if } 1\le q\le \lfloor n/2\rfloor,\\
2q-1, & \text{ if } \lfloor n/2\rfloor+1\le q\le n.
\end{cases}
\]
plain_text: Let $G = W(C_n)$ be the whisker graph of a cycle of length $n$. Then \[ \!(R/I(G)^ [q] ) = cases n+q-1, & if 1 q n/2,\\ 2q-1, & if n/2+1 q n. cases \]
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A concrete depth formula for whisker cycles; resolving it completes the depth classification for this natural family in combinatorial commutative algebra—important to specialists but limited broader consequences.
viability_rationale: Paper verifies the formula for 1≤q≤⌊n/2⌋ and one midpoint case; remaining finite explicit range q>n/2 seems amenable to targeted combinatorial/algebraic completion within a few years.
rationale: The paper states the conjecture from DRS24 and only verifies it in a substantial but restricted range (1 ≤ q ≤ ⌊n/2⌋, and an extra case when n odd), not for all q up to n.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture stated (lines 298–307). Paper: 'As a consequence of our main results, we verify this conjecture in a substantial range, namely for 1 ≤ q ≤ n/2, and additionally for q = ⌈n/2⌉ when n is odd' (lines 310–311).

title: Cohen-Macaulayness of squarefree powers of edge ideals of whisker graphs
plain_text: Let $G=W(C_n)$ be the whisker graph of the cycle $C_n$. Then \[ \!(R/I(G)^ [q] ) = cases n+q-1, & if 1 q n/2,\\ 2q-1, & if n/2<q n. cases \]
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Same whisker-cycle depth conjecture: verifies algebraic invariants in a natural graph family. Valuable within combinatorial commutative algebra, but scope is specialized rather than field-crossing.
viability_rationale: Significant partial results in this work (and prior), with an explicit remaining range. Techniques appear likely to be extendable to settle remaining q values soon.
rationale: The identical DRS24 conjecture is presented and the authors prove it only for certain ranges (Corollary shows 1≤q≤⌊n/2⌋ and one extra odd case), not the full q-range 1..n.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture statement (lines 1921–1933). Corollary verifies partial cases: 'depth = n+q-1 for 1≤q≤⌊n/2⌋; 2q-1 for n odd and q=⌈n/2⌉' (lines 1935–1947).

title: Combinatorics of the Cosmohedron

for $c = 0.005428\ldots$.

title: The $G$-Noncommutative Minimal Model Program
body_tex:
\label{conjecture:1}
Let $\pi:X\to Y$ be a $G$-contraction of a smooth projective variety $X$. 
\begin{itemize}
    \item[(A)] One can associate to $\pi$ a canonical family of quasi-convergent paths $\{\sigma_t^{\pi,\psi}\}$ in $\Stab(X_G)/\mathbb C$. Generic values of the parameter $\psi$ yield a $G$-linear semiorthogonal decomposition of $D^b_G(X)$, and different generic values give mutation-equivalent decompositions.
    \item[(B)] For a generic $\psi$, the semiorthogonal factors of $D^b_G(X)$ are closed under tensor product with complexes of the form $\pi^{\ast}(E)$ for $E\in\mathrm{Perf}_G(Y)$.
    \item[(C)] Given a further $G$-contraction $Y\to Y'$, the semiorthogonal decomposition of $D^b_G(X)$ associated with $X\to Y'$ refines the decomposition associated with $X\to Y$.
    \item[(D)] If $\pi:X\to X'$ is a $G$-equivariant morphism of smooth varieties with $R\pi_{\ast}(\cO_X)=\cO_{X'}$, then for suitable parameters, the semiorthogonal decomposition of $D^b_G(X)$ asscoiated to $X\to Y$ refines the decomposition obtained by combining the semiorthogonal decomposition of 
    \[\pi^{\ast}(D^b_G(X'))\cong D^b_G(X')\]
    associated to $X'\to Y$ with the decomposition 
    \[
    D^b_G(X)=\langle\mathrm{ker}(\pi_{\ast}),\pi^{\ast}(D^b_G(X'))\rangle.
    \]
\end{itemize}
plain_text: Let $:X Y$ be a $G$-contraction of a smooth projective variety $X$. itemize One can associate to $$ a canonical family of quasi-convergent paths $\ _t^ , \ $ in $(X_G)/ C$. Generic values of the parameter $$ yield a $G$-linear semiorthogonal decomposition of $D^b_G(X)$, and different generic values give mutation-equivalent decompositions. For a generic $$, the semiorthogonal factors of $D^b_G(X)$ are closed under tensor product with complexes of the form $^ (E)$ for $E_G(Y)$. Given a further $G$-contraction $Y Y'$, the semiorthogonal decomposition of $D^b_G(X)$ associated with $X Y'$ refines the decomposition associated with $X Y$. If $:X X'$ is a $G$-equivariant morphism of smooth varieties with $R_ (_X)=_ X' $, then for suitable parameters, the semiorthogonal decomposition of $D^b_G(X)$ asscoiated to $X Y$ refines the decomposition obtained by combining the semiorthogonal decomposition of \[^ (D^b_G(X')) D^b_G(X')\] associated to $X' Y$ with the decomposition \[ D^b_G(X)=(_ ),^ (D^b_G(X')). \] itemize
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A broad structural proposal linking equivariant Bridgeland stability, canonical quasi-convergent paths, and G-linear semiorthogonal decompositions; confirming it would have significant consequences across equivariant/noncommutative algebraic geometry.
viability_rationale: A broad equivariant extension of a deep noncommutative MMP/Bridgeland-stability program; only low-dimensional support so far and substantial conceptual obstacles remain in higher dimensions.
rationale: The paper formulates this G-equivariant NMMP proposal (items A–D) as a conjecture and provides partial evidence (e.g. support in two-dimensional case) but does not prove the full conjecture.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture: Let π:X→Y be a G-contraction... (A)–(D) give canonical quasi-convergent paths and SOD/refinement properties. 'A recent work [ESS2025] provides strong support for this conjecture in the two-dimensional case.'

title: A very robust Ramsey theorem for matchings
body_tex:
\label{conj:hyper}
  Let $q,s\ge 1$ and $r\ge 2$ be integers.
  There exists $\phi=\phi_{q,r}:(0,1]\to(0,\infty)$
  with $\phi(x)\to 0$ as $x\to 0$
  such that the following holds:
  For all $\vt\in\NN_+^q$ and $n\ge(r-1)\tmax+\Lambda_\vt+1+n\phi(s/n)$,
  if $H$ is an $r$-uniform $n$-vertex $s$-connector
  then $H\to \vt K_r^r$.
plain_text: Let $q,s 1$ and $r 2$ be integers. There exists $=_ q,r :(0,1](0,)$ with $(x) 0$ as $x 0$ such that the following holds: For all $_+^q$ and $n(r-1)+_+1+n(s/n)$, if $H$ is an $r$-uniform $n$-vertex $s$-connector then $H K_r^r$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Strong hypergraph generalisation of Alon–Frankl–Lovász and a robust Ramsey-type statement; a central extremal-combinatorics problem with clear structural consequences if resolved.
viability_rationale: Natural but ambitious hypergraph generalisation of known hard Ramsey/AF L theorem; no decisive local reductions given. Likely difficult and less probable to be settled within five years.
rationale: The authors explicitly propose this hypergraph generalisation as a conjecture (Conjecture 1425–1433) and do not prove it, indicating it is an open question in the paper.
evidence_snippet: Lines 1425-1433: 'We propose ... an r-uniform s-connector ... Conjecture: Let q,s≥1 and r≥2. There exists φ_{q,r} ... such that ... if H is an r-uniform n-vertex s-connector then H→ v K_r^r.'

title: A Geometric Approach to the Links-Quivers Correspondence II: Rational Links
plain_text: Given a link $L$, there is a symmetric quiver $Q_L$ with adjacency matrix $Q$ such that equation P(L)=P_ Q_L (x)|_ x_i (-1)^ Q_ ii -S_i q^ S_i-1 a^ A_i x , equation Where $S$ and $A$ can be thought of as linear forms and $Q$ as a quadratic form on an appropriately defined free $Z$-module of dimension given by the number of vertices in $Q$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Central Links-Quivers conjecture connecting colored HOMFLY-PT generating functions to quiver Hall/DT data. Proven for rational links but open generally; solving it would have deep structural and categorical consequences across knot homology and representation theory.
viability_rationale: General Links-Quivers for arbitrary links is broad and open; proved for rational links (SW21). Active progress exists but full generalization remains hard; modest near-term solvability likelihood.
rationale: The statement is the general Links-Quivers Correspondence for arbitrary links. The paper and cited SW21 only prove the rational-link case; the full conjecture remains unresolved.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture stated for arbitrary link L (lines 714–720). Paper notes Stošić and Wedrich prove the correspondence for rational links (line 724).

title: A Geometric Approach to the Links-Quivers Correspondence II: Rational Links

viability_rationale: Same global Links-Quivers statement (alternate normalization). Proven for rational links; general case still wide and nontrivial despite active work, so near-term resolution unlikely but possible progress.
rationale: This is the general Links-Quivers Correspondence phrased for P(L). Only rational links are proved in cited work; the general statement remains open.

title: A Geometric Approach to the Links-Quivers Correspondence I: Rational Tangles
body_tex:
Given a link $L$ with $|L|$ components, if we set 
   \[
   P(L)=\sum_{j\geq 0}\left(\prod_{i=1}^j(1-q^{2i})\right)^{|L|-2}P^{\bigwedge^j}_L(q,a)x^j,
   \]
   then there is a symmetric quiver $Q_L$ such that $P(L)$ and $P_{Q_L}(\overline{x})$ are equal after a change of variables.
plain_text: Given a link $L$ with $|L|$ components, if we set \[ P(L)=_ j 0 (_ i=1 ^j(1-q^ 2i ))^ |L|-2 P^ ^j _L(q,a)x^j, \] then there is a symmetric quiver $Q_L$ such that $P(L)$ and $P_ Q_L (x)$ are equal after a change of variables.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Restatement of the Links-Quivers correspondence for tangles/links: asserts finite quiver data recovers colored HOMFLY-PT generating functions. A central structural conjecture with broad implications for knot invariants and quiver DT theory.
viability_rationale: Restatement of the general Links-Quivers correspondence for all links. Given proof for rational tangles/links but broad scope, full proof appears unlikely within five years without new breakthroughs.
rationale: Same general Links-Quivers Correspondence for arbitrary links as in other items. The paper and cited SW21 address rational tangles/links only; the full conjecture stays open.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture statement for arbitrary link L (lines 205–211). The introduction notes Stošić and Wedrich proved the conjecture for rational links (lines 213–224).

title: Cyclic sieving for a class of rectangular domino tableaux
body_tex:
The set of rectangular domino tableaux $\DT(n^{2k})$ exhibits the cyclic sieving phenomenon with function
$$f(q)= 
\dfrac{[k \lceil n/2\rceil]!_q \hspace{1 mm} \cdot \hspace{1 mm} [k \lfloor n/2\rfloor]!_q}{ \displaystyle \prod_{i=1}^k [i]_q[i+1]_q\cdots \left[i+\left\lceil \frac{n}{2}\right\rceil -1\right]_q \cdot [i]_q[i+1]_q\cdots \left[i +\left\lfloor \frac{n}{2}\right\rfloor-1\right]_q} \cdot \displaystyle\qbin{kn}{k \lfloor n/2 \rfloor}_q.
$$.
plain_text: The set of rectangular domino tableaux $(n^ 2k )$ exhibits the cyclic sieving phenomenon with function $$f(q)= [k n/2]!_q 1 mm 1 mm [k n/2]!_q _ i=1 ^k [i]_q[i+1]_q _q [i]_q[i+1]_q _q k n/2 _q. $$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A natural CSP extension for rectangular domino tableaux; of clear interest in algebraic combinatorics and representation theory but limited in scope and broader mathematical consequences.
viability_rationale: Authors have exact enumerations and analogy to the proven rectangular SYT CSP; plausible but requires new structural/representation-theoretic understanding of domino promotion—nontrivial, so moderate near-term solvability.
rationale: Authors pose an explicit cyclic sieving conjecture for rectangular domino tableaux in the open problems section and do not prove it in the paper.
evidence_snippet: Lines 1144–1148: 'The set of rectangular domino tableaux DT(n^{2k}) exhibits the cyclic sieving phenomenon with function f(q)= ...' presented as a conjecture.

title: Remarks on the inverse Littlewood conjecture
body_tex:
\label{conj-1}
Let $A\subset \bbz$ be a finite set of size $N$. If $\anorm{1_A}_1\leq K\log N$ then for some $m,r\ll_K 1$ the following holds. There are finite arithmetic progressions $P_1,\ldots,P_m\subset \mathbb{Z}$, each of length at most $N$, and finite sets $X_1,\ldots,X_r\subset \bbz$ such that $\big| \bigcup_{j}X_j\big|=o_K(N)$,
together with $\epsilon_1,\ldots,\epsilon_m,\eta_1,\ldots,\eta_r\in \{-1,1\}$ such that
\[1_A=\sum_{1\leq i\leq m}\epsilon_i 1_{P_i}+\sum_{1\leq j\leq r}\eta_j 1_{X_j}.\]
plain_text: Let $A $ be a finite set of size $N$. If $1_A_1 K N$ then for some $m,r_K 1$ the following holds. There are finite arithmetic progressions $P_1,,P_m Z$, each of length at most $N$, and finite sets $X_1,,X_r $ such that $| _ j X_j|=o_K(N)$, together with $_1,,_m,_1,,_r \ -1,1\ $ such that \[1_A=_ 1 i m _i 1_ P_i +_ 1 j r _j 1_ X_j .\]
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A strong inverse-structure statement implying A is essentially a bounded signed union of few progressions (up to o(N)). Central in additive combinatorics with finite-field analogues; resolving it would have substantial structural consequences.
viability_rationale: Finite-field analogues and partial structure exist, but the strong integer decomposition into bounded many arithmetic progressions with only o_K(N) error is a substantial strengthening and unlikely to be resolved very soon.
rationale: Authors pose Conjecture 1 as a new speculation about structure and explicitly state they know of no counterexample; the paper does not prove or refute it.
evidence_snippet: Lines 388–394: "We do not know of a counterexample to the following conjecture. \begin{conjecture}\label{conj-1} ... If \|1_A\|_1\le K\log N then ... 1_A=\sum_{i}\epsilon_i1_{P_i}+\sum_j\eta_j1_{X_j}.\end{conjecture}"

title: Remarks on the inverse Littlewood conjecture
body_tex: Let $K>0$ and $N$ be sufficiently large depending only on $K$. Let $A\subset \bbz$ be a finite set of size $N$. If $\anorm{1_A}_1\leq K\log N$ then $\dim A\ll_K (\log N)^2$. Furthermore, $A$ contains a subset of size $\gg_K N$ and dimension $\ll_K \log N$.
plain_text: Let $K>0$ and $N$ be sufficiently large depending only on $K$. Let $A $ be a finite set of size $N$. If $1_A_1 K N$ then $ A_K ( N)^2$. Furthermore, $A$ contains a subset of size $_K N$ and dimension $_K N$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Sharpens Pichorides' dim bound from O((log N)^3) to O((log N)^2); examples already show (log N)^2 is attainable, so this is a natural, meaningful specialist conjecture within inverse Fourier/additive theory.
viability_rationale: Sharpens the known (log N)^3 bound to (log N)^2 with concrete examples suggesting optimality; quantitative nature and recent techniques make moderate near-term progress plausible but nontrivial to complete.

title: Generic flatness of the cohomology of thickenings
body_tex:
\label{our_conj}
Let $\ba\colonequals\left\{a_{ij} \mid 1\le i\le m,\, 0\le j \le n\right\}$ be indeterminates over a field $\kk$. Set $A\colonequals\kk[\ba]$, and let $R$ be the polynomial ring $A[x_0,\dots,x_n]$. Consider the ideal 
\[
I \colonequals \frakA_1 \cap \dots \cap \frakA_m
\]
of $R$, where $\frakA_i$ is the ideal of size $2$ minors
\[
\frakA_i \colonequals I_2 \begin{pmatrix}
x_0 & x_1 & \cdots & x_n\\
a_{i0} & a_{i1} & \cdots & a_{in}
\end{pmatrix}
\quad \text{ for } 1\le i\le m.
\] 
In other words, $I$ is the defining ideal of a \emph{generic} set of $m$ points in projective space. Set $\Rees(I)$ to be the Rees algebra
$\bigoplus_{t\ge 0} I^tT^t \subset R[T]$. Then there exists a nonzero element $a \in A$ such that 
\[
\HH^i_{(x_0,\dots,x_n)}\left(\Rees(I)\right)\otimes_A A_a
\]
is a free $A_a$-module for each $i\ge 0$.

This topology is unique in having the maximal value. Further, this same unlabeled topology has the largest number of tie-permitting labeled histories with exactly $z$ events, $ _r n z (n-1)/(r-1)$. This topology is not necessarily unique in having the maximal value. enumerate

title: On Pseudo-Effectivity and Volumes of Adjoint Classes in Kähler Families with Projective Central Fiber
body_tex:
\label{Transbpf}
Let $(X, B+\bbeta)$ be a generalized klt K\"ahler pair, where $X$ is a normal $\mathbb{Q}$-factorial compact K\"ahler variety, and $\alpha \in H_{\mathrm{BC}}^{1,1}(X,\mathbb{R})$ a nef class on $X$. If $\alpha-\left(K_X+B+ \bbeta_X\right)$ is nef and big, then there exists a proper surjective morphism with connected fibers $f: X \rightarrow Y$ to a normal compact K\"ahler variety $Y$ with rational singularities and a K\"ahler class $\omega_Y \in H_{\mathrm{BC}}^{1,1}(Y,\mathbb{R})$ such that $\alpha=f^* \omega_Y$.
plain_text: Let $(X, B+)$ be a generalized klt K\"ahler pair, where $X$ is a normal $Q$-factorial compact K\"ahler variety, and $ H_ BC ^ 1,1 (X,R)$ a nef class on $X$. If $-(K_X+B+ _X)$ is nef and big, then there exists a proper surjective morphism with connected fibers $f: X Y$ to a normal compact K\"ahler variety $Y$ with rational singularities and a K\"ahler class $_Y H_ BC ^ 1,1 (Y,R)$ such that $=f^* _Y$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Transcendental basepoint-free for generalized klt Kähler pairs; non-projective analogue of BCHM, central to the Kähler MMP and deformation/structural results in complex geometry.
viability_rationale: Transcendental BPF is proved for projective X and Kähler threefolds; higher-dimensional Kähler case remains open but is active — partial progress suggests modest chance within five years.
rationale: The paper states the transcendental base point free conjecture for Kähler varieties and only records it is known in the projective case; the full Kähler statement remains unresolved in general.

title: On the Graphical $r$-Stirling Numbers of the First Kind for Specific Graph Families
body_tex:
\label{conj:unimodal-planar}
For any planar graph $G$ of order $n$, the sequence of graphical Stirling numbers
\[
\{\st{G}{k}\}_{k=1}^n
\]
is \emph{unimodal}, i.e., there exists an index $k_0$ such that
\[
\st{G}{1} \le \cdots \le \st{G}{k_0} \ge \cdots \ge \st{G}{n}.
\]
This property is known for paths, cycles, trees, lollipop, and Tadpole graphs.
plain_text: For any planar graph $G$ of order $n$, the sequence of graphical Stirling numbers \[ \ G k \ _ k=1 ^n \] is unimodal, i.e., there exists an index $k_0$ such that \[ G 1 G k_0 G n . \] This property is known for paths, cycles, trees, lollipop, and Tadpole graphs.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Unimodality for all planar graphs is a natural, nontrivial enumerative-combinatorics conjecture over a large graph class; meaningful within combinatorics but not a cross-field flagship problem.
viability_rationale: Broad unimodality for all planar graphs is a wide generalization; known for special families but no clear finite reduction, so resolution within five years seems unlikely.
rationale: The unimodality for arbitrary planar graphs is posed as an open conjecture in the paper's 'Further Work and Open Problems' and is not proved here.
evidence_snippet: Lines 1789–1796: '\begin{conjecture} For any planar graph $G$ ... the sequence of graphical Stirling numbers \{${G\brack k}$\} is unimodal ... \end{conjecture}.'

title: On the Graphical $r$-Stirling Numbers of the First Kind for Specific Graph Families
body_tex:
\label{conj:real-rooted}
If $G$ is a chordal graph, then the graphical cycle polynomial of the first kind
\[
\mathcal{C}(G, x) = \sum_{k=1}^n \st{G}{k} x^k
\]
has only real roots. Real-rootedness would imply log-concavity and unimodality of the sequence $\{\st{G}{k}\}$.
plain_text: If $G$ is a chordal graph, then the graphical cycle polynomial of the first kind \[ C(G, x) = _ k=1 ^n G k x^k \] has only real roots. Real-rootedness would imply log-concavity and unimodality of the sequence $\ G k \ $.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Real-rootedness for chordal graphs' cycle polynomials would imply log-concavity/unimodality and links algebraic and structural graph properties—a significant specialist conjecture with clear structural consequences.
viability_rationale: Real-rootedness for chordal graphs is plausible and aligns with known techniques (induction on elimination), but nontrivial; moderate chance of progress but not imminent certainty.
rationale: The paper proposes real-rootedness for chordal graphs' graphical cycle polynomials as a conjecture without proof; it is presented as an open problem.
evidence_snippet: Lines 1802–1808: '\begin{conjecture} If $G$ is a chordal graph, then the graphical cycle polynomial ... has only real roots. Real-rootedness would imply log-concavity and unimodality. \end{conjecture}.'

title: A relation between the HOMFLY-PT and Kauffman polynomials via characters
body_tex:
If a knot $\mathcal{K}$ with braid index at least $m$, with $ m\geq4$, satisfies the HOMFLY--PT/Kauffman relation, then the HZ transform of its HOMFLY--PT polynomial is factorisable, i.e.
     \be\label{conj4}
 { H (\mathcal{K}) = \widehat {KF}(\mathcal{K}) \implies  Z(\mathcal{K}) = \frac{\lambda \prod_{i=0}^{m-2}(1-\lambda q^{\alpha_i})}{\prod_{i=0}^m (1-\lambda q^{\beta_i})} }.
 \ee
plain_text: If a knot $K$ with braid index at least $m$, with $ m4$, satisfies the HOMFLY--PT/Kauffman relation, then the HZ transform of its HOMFLY--PT polynomial is factorisable, i.e. H (K) = KF (K) Z(K) = _ i=0 ^ m-2 (1- q^ _i ) _ i=0 ^m (1- q^ _i ) .
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Relates two major knot polynomials via BMW algebra characters and HZ-factorisability; nontrivial structural link in knot invariants with consequences in representation theory and topological string contexts, but primarily subfield-level impact.
viability_rationale: Authors give explicit BMW-character conditions, computational evidence, and families proved; but a full uniform proof over all knots is nontrivial. Moderate chance of resolution within five years.
rationale: The paper states the corrected conjecture explicitly (for m≥4) and presents counterexamples to a previous version but does not prove the corrected claim; it is posed as an open expectation.
evidence_snippet: Lines 1286-1293: 'Hence, these knots provide counterexamples, disproving the right implication ... Therefore, we state the corrected version of the conjecture, which we expect to be true for general m≥4, as follows.'

title: Joint distribution of leftmost digits in positional notation and Schanuels's conjecture
body_tex:
\label{conj1}
 $\ln p_1,...,\ln p_m$ are algebraically independent.
Equivalently, the homomorphism 
$\psi : \mathbb Q(z_1,...,z_m) \mapsto \mathbb Q(\ln p_1,...,\ln p_m)$ 
satisfying $\psi(z_j) = \ln p_j$ is an isomorphism.
plain_text: $ p_1,..., p_m$ are algebraically independent. Equivalently, the homomorphism $ : Q(z_1,...,z_m) Q( p_1,..., p_m)$ satisfying $(z_j) = p_j$ is an isomorphism.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Algebraic independence of logarithms of distinct primes is a deep transcendence statement implied by Schanuel; resolving it would substantially advance transcendental number theory and algebraic independence problems.
viability_rationale: This is a special-case of Schanuel (algebraic independence of logs of primes). Deep transcendence problem with no concrete imminent route; very unlikely to be resolved in five years.
rationale: The conjecture (algebraic independence of ln p_i) is stated as an open conjecture and is only noted to follow from Schanuel's conjecture; it is not proved in the paper and is widely open.
evidence_snippet: Lines 351–356: "\ln p_1,...,\ln p_m are algebraically independent..."; Lemma (lines 359–367) states Schanuel's conjecture implies this, not a proof.

title: Hasse-Witt invariants of Calabi-Yau varieties
body_tex:
\rm
\label{17112025ymsc}
For all primes $p\not| N$,
the Hasse-Witt invariant ${\rm HW}(X_z,\alpha_z)$ up to sign  is the truncation of the holomorphic period at degree $p-1$  and  the quantity
$$
A_p(z):={\rm HP}(X_z,\alpha_z)^{p-1}
{\rm HW}(X_z,\alpha_z)
$$
after inserting the mirror map $A_p(z(q))$ has $p$-integral coefficients and modulo $p$ it is a  constant independent of $z$, that is, $A_p(z(q))\equiv_p 1$ (up to sign).
plain_text: For all primes $p| N$, the Hasse-Witt invariant $ HW (X_z,_z)$ up to sign is the truncation of the holomorphic period at degree $p-1$ and the quantity $$ A_p(z):= HP (X_z,_z)^ p-1 HW (X_z,_z) $$ after inserting the mirror map $A_p(z(q))$ has $p$-integral coefficients and modulo $p$ it is a constant independent of $z$, that is, $A_p(z(q))_p 1$ (up to sign).
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Relates Hasse–Witt invariants, truncated periods and p‑adic integrality after the mirror map; meaningful within arithmetic mirror symmetry and could have significant structural consequences in the subfield.
viability_rationale: Broad arithmetic/mirror-symmetry congruence; p-integrality of the mirror map is still unresolved except for hypergeometric cases. Partial cases likely progress, but full general resolution seems unlikely within five years.
rationale: Authors pose this Hasse–Witt vs period congruence as a conjecture and only provide numerical/experimental evidence; they explicitly note related p-integrality is an unsolved conjecture.
evidence_snippet: Lines 449-456 and 458: 'For all primes p\nmid N, the Hasse-Witt invariant ... is the truncation of the holomorphic period ... A_p(z)=HP^{p-1}HW ... A_p(z(q))\equiv_p 1. ... The p-integrality of the mirror map itself ... is still an unsolved conjecture.'

title: Algebraic statistics of Hüsler-Reiss graphical models in multivariate extremes
plain_text: For any graph $G$, the extremal ML degree is at most its Gaussian ML degree: equation (G) (G). equation
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Relates two ML-degree notions in algebraic statistics/matrix completion; equality known for chordal graphs and strict gaps occur. Resolving it would clarify structural links between Gaussian and extremal models.
viability_rationale: Substantial structural evidence and several resolved families (chordal, suspension, K_{2,n}, cycles) reduce the gap; remaining general cases likely technical but non-grand. Moderate chance of resolution within five years.
rationale: The paper states the inequality as an explicit conjecture and does not prove it; only special cases and examples are discussed.
evidence_snippet: Lines 1105-1111: "The examples above suggest the following conjecture... For any graph G, the extremal ML degree is at most its Gaussian ML degree: emld(G) ≤ mld(G)."

title: Algebraic statistics of Hüsler-Reiss graphical models in multivariate extremes
body_tex:
For any undirected graph $G$, it holds that
\[ \emlt(G) = \mathrm{MLT}(G)-1.  \]
plain_text: For any undirected graph $G$, it holds that \[ (G) = MLT(G)-1. \]
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A simple tight relation between extremal MLT and Gaussian MLT; if true it gives a clear bridge between inference thresholds for two model classes, with combinatorial and rigidity implications.
viability_rationale: Authors prove partial results (inequality, C4 case) and suggest matroid/rigidity methods, but an all-graph equality is broad and combinatorially challenging; plausible progress but not imminent within five years.
rationale: Authors pose equality emlt(G)=MLT(G)-1 as a conjecture and provide evidence (C4, inequalities) but do not prove it in general.
evidence_snippet: Lines 1297-1302: "Based on Theorem... we conjecture: For any undirected graph G, it holds that emlt(G) = MLT(G) - 1."

title: Coexact $1$-form spectral gaps of hyperbolic rational homology spheres
body_tex: There exists infinitely many hyperbolic rational homology spheres with $\lambda_1^*>2$.
plain_text: There exists infinitely many hyperbolic rational homology spheres with $_1^*>2$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Asserts infinitely many hyperbolic rational homology spheres with spectral gap above 2; has direct consequences for Floer theory (L-spaces) and 3-manifold spectral geometry, a central active problem.
viability_rationale: Authors produce infinite families with limits ≈0.82 and arithmetic examples, but current methods do not approach 2 and no concrete route to >2 is given; large remaining gap makes near-term resolution unlikely.
rationale: Paper proves families with limit points ≈0.82 and poses existence of infinitely many with λ1^*>2 as an explicit conjecture; not resolved here.
evidence_snippet: Lines 141-143: "We conjecture: There exists infinitely many hyperbolic rational homology spheres with λ1^* > 2."

title: Some properties of minimally nonperfectly divisible graphs
body_tex:
\label{coj-MNPD-nocliquecutset}
    A minimally nonperfectly divisible graph contains no clique cutset.
plain_text: A minimally nonperfectly divisible graph contains no clique cutset.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A structural graph-theory claim about MNPD graphs lacking clique cutsets. Useful for inductive proofs and class characterizations but narrow in scope.
viability_rationale: Recent 2025 conjecture with multiple partial verifications (P5-free, 4K1-free, 2P3-/claw-free cases) and structural reductions suggest moderate near-term solvability, but the general case remains broad and nontrivial.
rationale: The conjecture (attributed to Hoàng) is stated; the paper verifies it for several restricted graph classes but does not prove the general statement.
evidence_snippet: Lines 112-115: "Recently, Hoàng conjectured that an MNPD graph contains no clique cutset... \begin{conjecture} A minimally nonperfectly divisible graph contains no clique cutset."

title: The Poincaré polynomial of the type B analogue of $\overline{\mathcal{M}}_{0,n+1}$
body_tex: For all $n\geq 1$ the polynomials $B_n$ are real-rooted.
plain_text: For all $n 1$ the polynomials $B_n$ are real-rooted.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Real-rootedness of the type B Poincaré polynomials would imply gamma-positivity and strong log-concavity; verified computationally to n≤300, important in algebraic/combinatorial geometry.
viability_rationale: Gamma-positivity, a concrete quadratic recursion and verification to n≤300 give substantial progress and plausible routes (stability/interlacing), but a full general stability argument is nontrivial and not yet exhibited.
rationale: Authors propose B_n real-rooted for all n, verify up to n≤300 computationally, but do not prove the general claim.
evidence_snippet: Lines 887-892: "Conjecture: For all n≥1 the polynomials B_n are real-rooted. With the help of a computer we have verified that B_n is real-rooted for every n≤300."

title: On the expected value of energy in groups
body_tex:
\label{conj:EarthboundZero}
Let $G$ be a finitely generated group of exponential growth. Let $k \colon \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{N}$ be a nondecreasing function with $k \ll \sqrt{|\B_S(n)|}$, and let $\A$ be a $k$-subset of $F_n$ sampled uniformly at random in the word-balls $\B_S(n)$. Then,
\[
\lim_{n} \E[E(\A,\A)] = k^2 \,.
\]
plain_text: Let $G$ be a finitely generated group of exponential growth. Let $k N N$ be a nondecreasing function with $k |_S(n)|$, and let $$ be a $k$-subset of $F_n$ sampled uniformly at random in the word-balls $_S(n)$. Then, \[ _ n = k^2 \,. \]
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Predicts vanishing correction terms for expected energy in exponential-growth finitely generated groups, linking group growth with typical multiplicative behavior; meaningful in probabilistic group theory.
viability_rationale: Statement reduces to proving commuting and square-incidence probabilities vanish for all exponential-growth finitely generated groups. Proven for several classes (hyperbolic, RAAGs); a uniform general proof is nontrivial, so modest near-term solvability.
rationale: The limit statement for finitely generated groups of exponential growth is posed as a conjecture; the authors prove it only for certain classes.
evidence_snippet: Lines 1151-1157: "We thus propose the following conjecture... Let G be finitely generated of exponential growth... lim_n E[E(A,A)] = k^2."

title: On the expected value of energy in groups
body_tex:
\label{conj:Jeff}
Let $G$ be an infinite group and let $S$ be a finite generating set for $G$. Then, 
\begin{enumerate}[(i)]
\item $\iota_S(G)$ is positive if, and only if, $G$ is virtually abelian.
\item for every function $k \colon \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{N}$ such that $k \ll \sqrt[4]{|{\bf B}_S(n)|}$, and for every $\A$, $k$-subset of $G$ sampled uniformly at random in $\B_S(n)$, we have 
$$\E[E(\A, \A^{-1})] = 2  k^2 - k \,. $$
\end{enumerate}
plain_text: Let $G$ be an infinite group and let $S$ be a finite generating set for $G$. Then, enumerate[(i)] $_S(G)$ is positive if, and only if, $G$ is virtually abelian. for every function $k N N$ such that $k | B _S(n)| $, and for every $$, $k$-subset of $G$ sampled uniformly at random in $_S(n)$, we have $$ = 2 k^2 - k \,. $$ enumerate
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Two-part conjecture: involution density characterizes virtual abelianness and gives a universal expectation formula for E(A,A^{-1}). Both statements connect algebraic group structure with probabilistic invariants.
viability_rationale: Partial proofs for residually finite/subexponential, hyperbolic, lamplighter and finite-involution cases; residual-finiteness seems technical. Broad scope (all finitely generated groups including exotic examples) leaves nontrivial obstacles—plausible but not imminent.
rationale: The two-part conjecture for finitely generated groups is explicitly posed; paper proves parts in many cases but not in full generality.
evidence_snippet: Lines 1536-1543: "Conjecture: (i) ι_S(G) positive iff G virtually abelian. (ii) For k≪|B_S(n)|^{1/4}, E[E(A,A^{-1})]=2k^2-k."

title: Overcolored Partition Restricted by Parity of the Parts
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Specific congruences for overpartition counts modulo powers of two; interesting within partition arithmetic but a narrow, technical statement.
viability_rationale: Concrete infinite family with related modulo-p results in the paper; q-series/modular-form and 2-adic techniques plausibly extend. Infinite parameters add difficulty, so moderate chance of a breakthrough within five years.

title: A lift of the colored Jones polynomial of a knot
body_tex:
\label{conj.1g} 
There is a map
\be
\label{Jg}
\{ \text{Knots }K\text{ in} \,\, S^3\}
  \to \calH_{\BZ[t^{\pm 1}, \Delta_K^{\mathfrak{g}}(t)^{-1}]/\BZ[t^{\pm 1}]},
\qquad K \mapsto J^\mathfrak{g}_K(t,q) 
\ee
satisfying
\be
\label{eg}
J^{\mathfrak{g}}_K(q^\lambda,q)
= J^{\mathfrak{g}}_{K,\lambda}(q) \in \BZ[q^{\pm 1}]
\subset \calH_\BZ \hookrightarrow \BZ[\![q-1]\!],
\qquad (\text{for all dominant $\lambda$}) \,.
\ee
plain_text: There is a map \ Knots K in \,\, S^3\ _ / , K J^g_K(t,q) satisfying J^ g _K(q^,q) = J^ g _ K, (q) _ \!], (for all dominant $$) \,.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Proposes a Habiro-ring lift of colored Jones polynomials for all simple Lie algebras. Deeply bridges quantum invariants, integrality and Dehn-filling functoriality—high-impact in quantum topology.
viability_rationale: Authors prove the sl2 case and MMR holds for all simple g, so methods may extend. Nontrivial representation-theoretic/integrality obstacles remain; plausible progress in five years but requires substantial technical work.
rationale: The paper conjectures a Habiro-type lift for colored Jones polynomials for all simple Lie algebras; this general statement is not proved here.
evidence_snippet: Lines 728-745: "Conjecture: There is a map K↦J^g_K(t,q) to the Habiro ring satisfying J^g_K(q^λ,q)=J^g_{K,λ}(q) for all dominant λ."

viability_rationale: Proven for two-spin uniform and a broader class; conjecture is a natural extension and likely amenable to current large-deviation/optimization methods, though nonconvex obstacles remain.
rationale: Authors prove non-degeneracy for two-spin and a class of multi-spin systems but explicitly conjecture the uniform-mark multi-spin statement for all finite X; the paper does not prove it in full generality.
evidence_snippet: “We believe that this class should include all multi-spin systems with a uniform mark distribution, and in fact have the following conjecture.”; theorem proved only for |X|=2.

title: A Geometric Approach to the Links-Quivers Correspondence II: Rational Links

viability_rationale: Homological (Poincaré) refinement for rational knots is narrower; rational case benefits from concrete geometric/combinatorial control and recent partial results, making a 5-year resolution plausible but nontrivial.
rationale: This is a Poincaré-polynomial (homology-level) version for rational knots, posed as a conjecture in the paper and not proved here; it remains an open target.

title: KLR-Schur algebra of coherent sheaves on the projective line: Tilting and PBW bases
body_tex: For any $\alpha\in (\Z^2)^+$, the polynomial representation $P_{\alpha}$ is faithful.
plain_text: For any $ (^2)^+$, the polynomial representation $P_ $ is faithful.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Faithfulness of the polynomial representation would enable diagrammatic presentations and transfer known KLR/quiver techniques to the Coh(P1) Schur algebra; a meaningful specialist conjecture in geometric representation theory.
viability_rationale: Authors give limit/approximation to Kronecker-quiver Schur algebras, PBW bases, and known faithfulness for ν≠1/2; these concrete structural tools make extending faithfulness plausible within five years.
rationale: The authors explicitly formulate faithfulness of the polynomial representation P_α as a conjecture and state the proof for quiver contexts does not carry over, so it remains unresolved here.
evidence_snippet: This representation is known to be faithful in the context of quivers; the proof does not transpose to our setup but we conjecture that the same holds here: For any α∈(Z^2)^+, the polynomial representation P_α is faithful.

title: KLR-Schur algebra of coherent sheaves on the projective line: Tilting and PBW bases
body_tex:
\label{conj:PolyRep}
 For any $\alpha \in (\Z^2)^+$, the polynomial representation $P^{\frac12}_\alpha$ of $A^{\frac12}_\alpha$ is faithful.
plain_text: For any $ (^2)^+$, the polynomial representation $P^ 12 _$ of $A^ 12 _$ is faithful.

interestingness_rationale: The ν=1/2 case is a technically delicate instance whose faithfulness underpins PBW bases and explicit calculations; important locally but narrower than the general polynomial-representation faithfulness claim.
viability_rationale: The ν=1/2 case is delicate but the paper constructs PBW bases, stratifications, and polynomial models; technical but focused obstacles suggest a moderate near-term chance of resolution.
rationale: They conjecture faithfulness for the ν=1/2 polynomial representation; for ν≠1/2 faithfulness is known, but the ν=1/2 case is explicitly left open.
evidence_snippet: If ν≠1/2, from \cite[Proposition 4.4]{Przezdziecki2019} it follows that the polynomial representation P^{ν}_{α} is faithful. We conjecture that the same holds for ν=1/2.

title: Modularity of special cycles on Shimura varieties: a survey
body_tex: The classes $[\cZ(m)]$ are in fact defined in the $L^2$-cohomology of $X$, and their generating series is a modular form.
plain_text: The classes $[(m)]$ are in fact defined in the $L^2$-cohomology of $X$, and their generating series is a modular form.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Asserts modularity of special-cycle classes in L^2/intersection cohomology of general Shimura varieties; a broadly relevant structural statement linking compactifications, L^2-cohomology and Kudla-type modularity.
viability_rationale: Concrete L2/intersection-cohomology framework with existing proofs in key cases (Bruinier–Zuffetti, Getz–Goresky) gives a plausible path to wider resolution within several years.
rationale: The paper explicitly poses this as a conjecture and only cites partial results (orthogonal up to middle degree; Hilbert modular surfaces). No general proof or refutation is given.
evidence_snippet: Lines 659–663: 'The classes [\cZ(m)] are in fact defined in the L^2-cohomology of X, and their generating series is a modular form.' Cited partial results: Bruinier–Zuffetti and Getz–Goresky.

title: Modularity of special cycles on Shimura varieties: a survey
body_tex: Let $X$ be a Shimura variety for $\SU(2,2)\sim \SO(4,2)$ that is simultaneously unitary and orthogonal type. The generating series $\Phi_2(q)\in \CH^2(X)\otimes \Mod_3(\Sp_4(\Z))$ of codimension 2 orthogonal special cycles is the Saito-Kurokawa lift of the generating series $\Phi_1(q)\in \CH^2(X) \otimes \Mod_4(\SL_2(\Z))$ of codimension 2 unitary special cycles.
plain_text: Let $X$ be a Shimura variety for $(2,2) (4,2)$ that is simultaneously unitary and orthogonal type. The generating series $_2(q) ^2(X) _3(_4())$ of codimension 2 orthogonal special cycles is the Saito-Kurokawa lift of the generating series $_1(q) ^2(X) _4(_2())$ of codimension 2 unitary special cycles.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Predicts a concrete Saito–Kurokawa lift relating orthogonal and unitary generating series for the SU(2,2)/SO(4,2) case—an interesting, specialized bridge between lift theory and cycle-generating series.
viability_rationale: Low-dimensional coincidence with known lifting maps (Saito–Kurokawa) and recent modular interpretations suggest a feasible, targeted proof strategy for this specific isogeny case.
rationale: The paper explicitly states this as a conjecture in a survey and offers no proof; the exact lifting relation for these Chow-valued generating series is not established in the literature cited.
evidence_snippet: "Let X be a Shimura variety for SU(2,2) ~ SO(4,2). The generating series Φ_2(q) ... of codimension 2 orthogonal special cycles is the Saito-Kurokawa lift of the generating series Φ_1(q) ... of codimension 2 unitary special cycles."

title: Beyond singular value gaps in randomized subspace approximation
